name: MNE-Python (EEG/MEG Signal Processing)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); MNE developers / Martinos Center

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; GPU not supported natively; large EEG/MEG datasets CPU-bound

agent_readiness.notes: EEG/MEG signal processing and source localization. import mne. raw = mne.io.read_raw_edf('eeg.edf'). raw.filter(1, 40). raw.notch_filter(50). events = mne.find_events(raw). epochs = mne.Epochs(raw, events, event_id={'stim': 1}, tmin=-0.2, tmax=0.8). evoked = epochs.average(). ICA: ica = mne.preprocessing.ICA(n_components=20). ica.fit(raw). Source localization: forward model + inverse solution (MNE, dSPM, sLORETA). Connectivity: mne.connectivity. Plotting: topomap, ERP, time-frequency. 60+ file formats: EDF, BDF, FIF, EEG.

gotchas: Source localization requires FreeSurfer MRI processing
Steep learning curve — neuroscience concepts required
Memory usage with continuous recordings can be large
Some operations are slow without parallelism (n_jobs parameter)
best_when: EEG/MEG data preprocessing (filtering, artifact removal, ICA)
avoid_when: Real-time EEG (use BCI2000 or OpenBCI frameworks)
summary: MNE-Python is the standard Python library for EEG/MEG signal processing. raw.filter(1, 40).notch_filter(50). Epochs/Evoked for event-related analysis. ICA for artifact removal. Source localization (MNE, dSPM, sLORETA). Time-frequency: morlet wavelets. 60+ file formats. BSD-3. Excellent documentation. Best for EEG/MEG preprocessing, ERP, connectivity, and source analysis. Requires neuroscience background.
